description:
Compounding feels slow… until it suddenly isn’t.
That’s the part most people never make it to.

In this video, I explain why compounding feels painfully slow at the beginning, using the penny-doubling example, the Rule of 72, and my real brokerage account to show what’s actually happening under the surface.

I walk through a live portfolio update, show how staying fully invested, avoiding “dead cash,” and keeping momentum on changes the compounding curve — even during market volatility.

This isn’t theory.
This is real math, real numbers, and real compounding in motion.

What you’ll learn

✅ Why compounding feels invisible early on
✅ The penny-doubling example most people misunderstand
✅ How the Rule of 72 shows how fast money actually doubles
✅ Why being fully invested matters more than perfect timing
✅ How “dead cash” quietly slows compounding
✅ Net portfolio value vs gross portfolio value (and why both matter)
✅ How I track performance using time-weighted return vs historical values
✅ How I live out of my brokerage account while staying invested
✅ Why compounding only works if you don’t interrupt it

The core takeaway

Compounding doesn’t fail — people interrupt it.

Most investors quit during the quiet phase, right before the curve steepens.
The goal isn’t speed.
The goal is keeping compounding turned on long enough for math to take over.

Question for you (drop a comment 👇)
Why do you think compounding is so hard to stick with early on?
• It feels like nothing is happening
• Volatility messes with emotions
• Money isn’t always fully invested
• The timeline feels too long
• Something else

⏱️ Chapters
00:00 – Why Compounding Feels Slow
00:25 – Portfolio Update 
01:45 – Performance vs Benchmarks (Time-Weighted Return)
02:53 – Living Out of the Brokerage Account
04:47 – Compounding Mindset (Munger, Buffett, Graham)
06:15 – Penny Doubling Explained
07:57 – Rule of 72 (How Fast Money Doubles)
09:55 – Dead Cash & Momentum
11:44 – Traditional vs Fully Deployed Compounding
13:50 – Why Timelines Compress
16:03 – Proof Layer (IRR vs TWR)
20:26 – Community Poll Results
23:13 – Risk Management & Put Protection
24:49 – Final Thoughts + Spark to FIRE

🧠 Key Takeaways

• Momentum beats patience alone
• Fully invested beats perfectly timed
• Volatility doesn’t break compounding — panic does
